frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with needle visible being loaded.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper loading of the needle observed.

frame_analyses.description: Suture being placed through the marks on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Positioning seems accurate, slight adjustment might be needed.

frame_analyses.description: Beginning of the first throw observed.
Securing the double throw of the first knot.
Starting the second throw switching hands.
Third throw being initiated, hands switched.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switch observed, throw appears accurate.

frame_analyses.description: Final cut of suture ends completing the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ends cut cleanly within the trainer.

knot_assessments.notes: First knot is a secure surgeon's knot.
Second throw is secure with correct hand switch.
Third throw is secure, hand switch correct.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit fully closed, no visible gaps.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated solid technique with a secure triple knot. Hand switching was executed correctly, and the suture placement was accurate.
improvement_suggestions: Reduce suture deviation to under 1mm for higher precision.
Increase speed to lower overall completion time.
strengths: Solid knot tying technique with secure throws.
Consistent hand switching as required between throws.
